---
name: Amazon link TLD and host allowlist
description: Lessons learned about Amazon URL formats and SSRF allowlist for resolve_short_link
---

# Amazon Link Host Allowlist

## Rule
Use a regex-based check — not a fixed set — to allow Amazon URLs in `resolve_short_link`.

**Why:** Amazon owns the `.amazon` TLD. URLs like `https://link.amazon/...` (no `.com`) are valid Amazon short links generated by the Saudi app. A static allowlist will always miss edge cases.

## How to apply
The current implementation uses `_AMAZON_HOST_RE` regex:
```
r"(?:^|\.)amazon(?:\.[a-z]{2,6}(?:\.[a-z]{2,3})?)?$"
```
This covers: `amazon.sa`, `amazon.com`, `link.amazon.com`, `link.amazon.sa`, `link.amazon` (bare TLD), etc.

Known shorteners in `_SHORTENER_HOSTS`: `amzn.to`, `amzn.eu`, `a.co`, `ty.gl`, `bit.ly`, `tinyurl.com`, `t.co`, `rb.gy`.

## Key lesson
`link.amazon` (no TLD suffix) is Amazon's own `.amazon` gTLD — treat it as a trusted Amazon domain, not a suspicious URL.
